title: MCP-A action Response
description: Response for the action primitive (SPEC §action). `action_id` and `status` are always required. `clarification` is present only when status is `clarification_required`; `result`/`effects` when `completed`; `error` when `failed`. The action_id is stable across clarification rounds and usable with the explain primitive.

action_id.description: Opaque handle identifying this action. Stable across clarification rounds; usable with explain. MUST be present.

status.description: Outcome of this turn (SPEC §action). `clarification_required` = the service needs more info; `completed` = the action was executed; `failed` = execution failed.

summary.description: Human-readable account of what was done (when completed), what is still needed (when clarification_required), or what went wrong (when failed).

clarification.description: Present only when status is `clarification_required`. Describes the information the service still needs before it can execute.

needed.description: The fields the client MUST supply in a continuation request's `inputs` map.

prompt.description: Optional human-readable prompt summarizing what the client should provide.

result.description: Outcome payload of a completed action (e.g., the created record). Present only when status is `completed`.

effects.description: The state-changing effects the service applied. Present when status is `completed`.

citations.description: Optional sources supporting how the action was interpreted or which records it touched, each with at least source_system.

error.description: Present only when status is `failed`. The abstract MCP-A error describing the failure.

routing_decision.description: Routing/interpretation detail, present only when debug/explain was requested (see explain primitive).

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the action turn completed.
